issue: [GA4 | CONF-CONV-001] `page_view` is marked as a GA4 key event.
summary: Explanation: GA4 is counting a normal page load as a key event. A page view only shows that someone opened a page; it is not a purchase, lead, checkout step, or other business outcome. Why it matters: It can distort con...
detail:
Explanation:
GA4 is counting a normal page load as a key event. A page view only shows that someone opened a page; it is not a purchase, lead, checkout step, or other business outcome.

Why it matters:
It can distort conversion counts, ROAS, CPA, revenue reporting, and bidding decisions.

Recommended action:
Verify the event still appears as a normal event, but key-event status is OFF unless the business has intentionally defined it as a true conversion.

Verification standard:
1. Yes — confirm in GA4 Admin UI → Admin → Key Events that `page_view` key event toggle is on. 2. Yes — confirm in GA4 Admin UI → Admin → Key Events that `page_view` key event toggle is on. Done means the evidence now supports this target: `page_view` should not be a key event. Only intentional conversion actions (purchase, lead form submission) or deliberate micro-conversions should be key events. Page views are a traffic metric, not a business outcome. Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified. 3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: `page_view` should not be a key event. Only intentional conversion actions (purchase, lead form submission) or deliberate micro-conversions should be key events. Page views are a traffic metric, not a business outcome.
fix: Verify the event still appears as a normal event, but key-event status is OFF unless the business has intentionally defined it as a true conversion.
verify:
1. Yes — confirm in GA4 Admin UI → Admin → Key Events that `page_view` key event toggle is on.

Done means the evidence now supports this target: `page_view` should not be a key event. Only intentional conversion actions (purchase, lead form submission) or deliberate micro-conversions should be key events. Page views are a traffic metric, not a business outcome.

Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified.
3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: `page_view` should not be a key event. Only intentional conversion actions (purchase, lead form submission) or deliberate micro-conversions should be key events. Page views are a traffic metric, not a business outcome.
routingNote: Official GA4 docs say key events should measure important actions; Google’s key-event tutorial explicitly says not to mark page_view itself because it makes every pageview a key event.
businessIssue: page view is being counted as a business success action.
businessDetail:
page view is being counted as a business success action.

analytics is counting a normal page load as an important business action. A page view only shows that someone opened a page; it is not a purchase, lead, checkout step, or other business outcome.
It can distort important business result counts, ad return, cost per result, revenue reporting, and bidding decisions.
businessFix:
What to do in business terms:
Verify the tracked action still appears as a normal tracked action, but key-tracked action status is OFF unless the business has intentionally defined it as a true important business result.

Keep it simple: confirm the problem is still true, fix the setting or tracking source, then record proof so the team can trust the result later.
businessVerify:
How to know it is fixed:
1. Yes — confirm in analytics Admin UI  to  Admin  to  important business action that page view important business action toggle is on.

Done means the evidence now supports this target: page view should not be an important business action. Only intentional important business result actions (purchase, lead form submission) or deliberate micro-important business result should be important business action. Page views are a traffic reporting number, not a business outcome.

Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified.
3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: page view should not be an important business action. Only intentional important business result actions (purchase, lead form submission) or deliberate micro-important business result should be important business action. Page views are a traffic reporting number, not a business outcome.

Do not mark this complete from memory. Mark it complete only after you see proof and save the evidence.

issue: [GA4 | CONF-CONV-002] `session_start` is marked as a GA4 key event.
summary: Explanation: GA4 is counting the automatic session_start event as a key event. This only means a visit began, so it should not be used as a success metric. Why it matters: It can distort conversion counts, ROAS, CPA, re...
detail:
Explanation:
GA4 is counting the automatic session_start event as a key event. This only means a visit began, so it should not be used as a success metric.

Why it matters:
It can distort conversion counts, ROAS, CPA, revenue reporting, and bidding decisions.

Recommended action:
Verify the event still appears as a normal event, but key-event status is OFF unless the business has intentionally defined it as a true conversion.

Verification standard:
1. Yes — confirm in GA4 Admin UI that `session_start` key event toggle is on. 2. Yes — confirm in GA4 Admin UI that `session_start` key event toggle is on. Done means the evidence now supports this target: `session_start` should not be a key event. It is an automatically collected event used for session counting, not a business outcome. Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified. 3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: `session_start` should not be a key event. It is an automatically collected event used for session counting, not a business outcome.
fix: Verify the event still appears as a normal event, but key-event status is OFF unless the business has intentionally defined it as a true conversion.
verify:
1. Yes — confirm in GA4 Admin UI that `session_start` key event toggle is on.

Done means the evidence now supports this target: `session_start` should not be a key event. It is an automatically collected event used for session counting, not a business outcome.

Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified.
3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: `session_start` should not be a key event. It is an automatically collected event used for session counting, not a business outcome.
routingNote: Official GA4 docs say key events should measure important actions; Google’s key-event tutorial explicitly says not to mark page_view itself because it makes every pageview a key event.
businessIssue: session start is being counted as a business success action.
businessDetail:
session start is being counted as a business success action.

analytics is counting the automatic session start tracked action as an important business action. This only means a visit began, so it should not be used as a success reporting number.
It can distort important business result counts, ad return, cost per result, revenue reporting, and bidding decisions.
businessFix:
What to do in business terms:
Verify the tracked action still appears as a normal tracked action, but key-tracked action status is OFF unless the business has intentionally defined it as a true important business result.

Keep it simple: confirm the problem is still true, fix the setting or tracking source, then record proof so the team can trust the result later.
businessVerify:
How to know it is fixed:
1. Yes — confirm in analytics Admin UI that session start important business action toggle is on.

Done means the evidence now supports this target: session start should not be an important business action. It is an automatically collected tracked action used for session counting, not a business outcome.

Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified.

issue: [GA4 | CONF-CUST-004] The native `itemCategory` dimension is 100% blank across all ecommerce events.
summary: Explanation: The ecommerce payload is incomplete or not using clean GA4 field names. Product, variant, category, price, discount, coupon, tax, shipping, or transaction data may be missing, inconsistent, or stored in leg...
detail:
Explanation:
The ecommerce payload is incomplete or not using clean GA4 field names. Product, variant, category, price, discount, coupon, tax, shipping, or transaction data may be missing, inconsistent, or stored in legacy fields.

Why it matters:
It makes product, collection, promotion, checkout, and revenue reports incomplete or misleading.

Recommended action:
Fix the source configuration or implementation, then document before/after evidence.

Verification standard:
1. Yes — verify whether the Shopify GA4 app or native Google channel is sending the `item_category` parameter. Check via GTM Preview or Shopify's GA4 event inspector. 2. Yes — verify whether the Shopify GA4 app or native Google channel is sending the `item_category` parameter. Check via GTM Preview or Shopify's GA4 event inspector. Done means the evidence now supports this target: Each Shopify product should map to at least one category. Expected values (based on observed page paths and product names): Organic Oils (زيوت عضوية), Essential Oils (زيوت عطرية), Body Butter (زبدة الجسم), Glycerin Soap (صابون الجلسرين), Hydrosol (هيدرسول), Natural Extracts (مستخلصات طبيعية), Product Sets (مجموعات ثراء), etc. `itemCategory2` and `itemCategory3` should carry subcategory breadcrumbs. Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified. 3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: Each Shopify product should map to at least one category. Expected values (based on observed page paths and product names): Organic Oils (زيوت عضوية), Essential Oils (زيوت عطرية), Body Butter (زبدة الجسم), Glycerin Soap (صابون الجلسرين), Hydrosol (هيدرسول), Natural Extracts (مستخلصات طبيعية), Product Sets (مجموعات ثراء), etc. `itemCategory2` and `itemCategory3` should carry subcategory breadcrumbs.
fix: Use the original fix, but verify in the platform first and save before/after evidence.
verify:
1. Yes — verify whether the Shopify GA4 app or native Google channel is sending the `item_category` parameter. Check via GTM Preview or Shopify's GA4 event inspector.

Done means the evidence now supports this target: Each Shopify product should map to at least one category. Expected values (based on observed page paths and product names): Organic Oils (زيوت عضوية), Essential Oils (زيوت عطرية), Body Butter (زبدة الجسم), Glycerin Soap (صابون الجلسرين), Hydrosol (هيدرسول), Natural Extracts (مستخلصات طبيعية), Product Sets (مجموعات ثراء), etc. `itemCategory2` and `itemCategory3` should carry subcategory breadcrumbs.

Add a screenshot, export link, or note in your tracker before marking the row Verified.
3. Confirm the final state matches this expected condition: Each Shopify product should map to at least one category. Expected values (based on observed page paths and product names): Organic Oils (زيوت عضوية), Essential Oils (زيوت عطرية), Body Butter (زبدة الجسم), Glycerin Soap (صابون الجلسرين), Hydrosol (هيدرسول), Natural Extracts (مستخلصات طبيعية), Product Sets (مجموعات ثراء), etc. `itemCategory2` and `itemCategory3` should carry subcategory breadcrumbs.
